skill.name: run-nx-generator
skill.description: Run Nx generators with prioritization for workspace-plugin generators. Use this when generating code, scaffolding new features, or automating repetitive tasks in the monorepo.
skill.summary: Run Nx generators with workspace-plugin prioritization and verification steps.
skill.author: nrwl

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many Markdown backticks and generator wording. The weak cryptography findings are false positives, but the skill intentionally guides shell commands that can modify a workspace.

medium_findings.title: Workspace-Modifying Shell Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to run Nx generator, test, and formatting commands. This is legitimate for an Nx workflow, but generators can create or change files and execute local project generator logic.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and match the stated purpose of the skill. Risk comes from normal generator side effects, not from evidence of malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static False Positive
low_findings.description: The flagged lines use the word generator in documentation and command examples. No evidence found of cryptographic APIs, hashing algorithms, encryption, or credential handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are plain Markdown documentation for Nx generators. There is no crypto code or weak algorithm usage in the file.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Static False Positives
low_findings.description: Several static findings report Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the file is Markdown documentation. Inline backticks name commands, paths, tools, and flags rather than execute code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context shows Markdown formatting rather than Ruby code. Some fenced command examples remain relevant as user-run shell guidance and are covered separately.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Guidance
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes direct shell command examples for Nx generation, affected tests, and Prettier formatting. Users should review generated changes before applying them.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are clearly present and intentionally part of the workflow. They are not malicious, but they can modify local files.

content.value_statement: Nx monorepos often contain both internal and core generators, which makes the right choice unclear. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through generator selection, execution, and verification.
actual_capabilities: Prioritizes workspace-plugin generators before core Nx generators.
Guides schema lookup before running a selected generator.
Provides command patterns for Nx generator execution.
Recommends dry runs before applying generated changes.
Prompts verification with affected tests and formatting.
limitations: It does not validate generator safety beyond normal repository review.
It depends on Nx and workspace generators being available.
It does not choose options without project context.
It can produce workspace changes that require manual review.

use_cases.title: Scaffold a Monorepo Feature
use_cases.description: Select the best internal generator and run it with the required options.

use_cases.title: Automate Internal Version Updates
use_cases.description: Use a workspace-plugin generator for repeatable updates such as Maven version changes.

use_cases.title: Create Nx Plugin Infrastructure
use_cases.description: Generate plugin scaffolding while following the repository generator priority rules.

prompt_templates.title: Find the Right Generator
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the run-nx-generator skill to identify the best generator for creating a new feature in this Nx workspace.

prompt_templates.title: Preview a Generator Run
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the run-nx-generator skill to inspect the generator schema, choose options, and preview the command with a dry run.

prompt_templates.title: Apply and Verify Changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the run-nx-generator skill to run the selected generator, format the generated files, and identify affected tests.

prompt_templates.title: Plan an Internal Tooling Update
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the run-nx-generator skill to update internal tooling with a workspace-plugin generator and define the verification steps.

output_examples.input: Create a new React library in the Nx workspace.
output_examples.output: The assistant checks workspace-plugin generators first, reviews the chosen schema, proposes a dry run, then lists verification steps.

output_examples.input: Bump the Maven plugin version across the repository.
output_examples.output: The assistant selects the internal Maven version generator, identifies required options, and recommends affected tests after generation.
The assistant uses the workspace-plugin generator path, explains the name option, and prompts review of generated files.

best_practices: Run a dry run before applying generator output.
Prefer workspace-plugin generators when they match the task.
Review generated files before running broad formatting or tests.
anti_patterns: Running core Nx generators before checking internal workspace generators.
Applying generator output without reading the schema options.
Committing generated changes without formatting and test verification.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It guides generator selection and execution in an Nx monorepo.

faq.question: Does it prefer internal generators?
faq.answer: Yes. It prioritizes workspace-plugin generators before standard Nx generators.

faq.question: Can it run commands?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides shell command guidance for Nx generation, tests, and formatting.

faq.question: Is a dry run recommended?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill recommends using dry runs to preview changes before applying them.

faq.question: Does it verify generated code?
faq.answer: It recommends reviewing files, running affected tests, and formatting generated code.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
